title: Transform: Type, Value, constructors
analysis: Add Transform(usize) to the Type enum and a corresponding Value::Transform { rotation, translation } variant. Transform represents a rigid-body transformation: rotation (Orientation<N>) + translation (Vector<N,Length>). This follows the exact same compound-type pattern as Frame { origin: Box<Value>, basis: Box<Value> }, but with different semantics and fields: Transform stores a rotation (Orientation) and a translation (Vector) rather than an origin (Point) and a basis (Orientation). Two constructors go in reify-stdlib: transform3(rotation, translation) and transform3_identity(). The implementation touches 3 files: ty.rs (Type enum + Display + factory + tests), value.rs (Value enum + PartialEq + Ord + content_hash + Display + dimension + tests), and stdlib lib.rs (eval_builtin match arms + tests). All trait impls follow the Frame pattern exactly. Transform gets type_tag=21 (next after Frame=20) and content_hash tag=21 (next available after Frame's tag=20). The Ord comment on line 460 must be extended to include Transform=21.

steps.description: Write failing tests for Type::Transform(usize) in ty.rs mod tests: construction and equality (Transform(3)==Transform(3), Transform(3)!=Transform(2)), Display ('Transform3', 'Transform2'), factory method Type::transform(n), Eq/Hash consistency via HashMap, is_numeric()=false, as_name()=None, Transform(3)!=Frame(3), Transform(3)!=Orientation(3)

Add Transform(usize) variant to Type enum in ty.rs (after Frame(usize) on line 49). Add Display arm: 'Transform{n}' (after Frame arm on line 810). Add factory method Type::transform(n: usize) -> Self (after frame factory on line 120). is_numeric already returns false via catch-all, as_name already returns None. All step-1 tests pass.

Write failing tests for Value::Transform in value.rs mod tests: construction with Orientation rotation and Vector translation (verify fields via destructure), PartialEq (equal transforms, different rotation, different translation), Display format 'transform([w,x,y,z]q, vec(...))', dimension() returns DIMENSIONLESS, content_hash determinism (same transform same hash), content_hash distinct from Frame and Orientation and Vector, Ord type_tag=21 > Frame=20, same-type Ord compares rotation first then translation, content_hash with neg-zero in translation differs

Add Value::Transform { rotation: Box<Value>, translation: Box<Value> } variant to Value enum (after Frame on line 62). Implement: content_hash arm (tag=21, combine rotation+translation hashes following Frame's tag=20 pattern on line 322-324), PartialEq arm (rotation==rotation && translation==translation following Frame pattern on lines 414-417), Ord type_tag=21 (update comment on line 460, add tag match on ~line 483), same-variant cmp rotation.cmp then translation.cmp (after Frame arm on line 584-586), Display arm 'transform({rotation}, {translation})' (after Frame arm on line 717-719). Dimension returns DIMENSIONLESS via existing catch-all on line 180. All step-3 tests pass.

Write failing tests for transform3(rotation, translation) constructor in stdlib lib.rs: valid Orientation + Vector3<Length> args returns Value::Transform with correct rotation and translation fields, verify fields stored exactly, wrong arg count (0, 1, 3 args) returns Undef, non-Orientation first arg (e.g. Real) returns Undef, non-Vector second arg (e.g. Real, Point3, Orientation) returns Undef, Vector2 translation (wrong component count) returns Undef, dimensionless Vector3 (Real components) is accepted

Add 'transform3' match arm to eval_builtin in stdlib lib.rs (after frame3 block around line 513): validate exactly 2 args, first must be Value::Orientation { .. }, second must be Value::Vector with exactly 3 components. Return Value::Transform { rotation: Box::new(args[0].clone()), translation: Box::new(args[1].clone()) }. Return Value::Undef on any validation failure. All step-5 tests pass.

Write failing tests for transform3_identity() constructor in stdlib lib.rs: no-arg call returns Value::Transform, rotation field equals identity Orientation (w:1,x:0,y:0,z:0), translation field equals zero Vector3<Length> vec([0m,0m,0m]), call with any args (1 arg, 2 args) returns Undef

Add 'transform3_identity' match arm to eval_builtin in stdlib lib.rs (after transform3 block): if args.is_empty(), return Value::Transform { rotation: Box::new(Orientation{w:1,x:0,y:0,z:0}), translation: Box::new(Vector([Value::length(0.0) x3])) }; else return Value::Undef. All step-7 tests pass.

design_decisions.decision: Transform stores rotation (Orientation) + translation (Vector), NOT origin (Point) + basis (Orientation) like Frame
design_decisions.rationale: Transform represents a rigid-body transformation (how to move/rotate something), while Frame represents a coordinate system (where something is positioned and oriented). The task description specifies rotation + translation, matching standard SE(3) representation where T(x) = R*x + t.

design_decisions.decision: Value::Transform uses Box<Value> for both fields, following the Frame pattern
design_decisions.rationale: Frame already established the compound-type pattern with Box<Value> fields (line 62 of value.rs). Consistency matters more than marginal optimization. Enables delegation for Display/content_hash/comparison.

design_decisions.decision: type_tag=21 and content_hash tag=21 for Transform
design_decisions.rationale: Frame uses type_tag=20 and hash tag=20. Next sequential value ensures no hash or ordering collisions. The Ord comment on line 460 documents the full tag mapping and must be extended.

In rigid-body transforms, the mathematical convention is T(x) = R*x + t, with rotation applied first. The rotation comes first both mathematically and in the constructor signature, matching how frame3 takes (origin, basis) in logical order.

design_decisions.decision: transform3_identity uses zero-length Vector3 (0m, 0m, 0m) not dimensionless Vector3
design_decisions.rationale: Consistent with frame3_identity which uses Value::length(0.0) for origin components. Translation is inherently a length-dimensioned quantity in the physical world.

design_decisions.decision: Display format is 'transform(rot, trans)' following 'frame(origin, basis)' pattern
design_decisions.rationale: Existing compound type Frame uses lowercase function-call notation for Display (line 718). Transform follows the same convention for consistency.

design_decisions.decision: transform3 accepts any Vector3 regardless of inner component dimension
design_decisions.rationale: Consistent with frame3 which accepts any Point3 regardless of inner quantity dimension (line 502 just checks component count, not dimension). Users might use dimensionless coordinates for pure geometric operations.

reuse.how: Transform(usize) follows the identical pattern — single usize parameter, Display as 'Transform{n}', factory method Type::transform(n), is_numeric returns false via catch-all, as_name returns None via catch-all.

reuse.what: Value::Frame { origin, basis } compound value pattern
reuse.how: Transform's trait impls follow Frame's structure exactly: content_hash uses unique tag and combines sub-hashes; PartialEq delegates to inner field equality; Ord compares fields in order (rotation then translation); Display uses function-call notation.
Copy Frame tests, rename Frame→Transform, verify same behavioral properties (construction, Display, factory, Hash, is_numeric, as_name, ne to other types).
Copy Frame value tests, adapt to Transform fields (rotation+translation instead of origin+basis), adjust expected Display format, update type_tag comparison (21>20 instead of 20>19).

Copy frame3 constructor, change: first arg validates as Orientation (matches! Orientation { .. }), second validates as Vector with 3 components. Identity returns Orientation{1,0,0,0} + Vector([length(0),length(0),length(0)]).
Copy frame3 test suite, adapt to transform3 argument types (Orientation first, Vector second) and expected Value::Transform output variant.
Reuse directly as the rotation argument for transform3 tests.
